input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI ContentPage called SettingsPage.xaml that currently uses
plain bindings with no x:DataType. The page's BindingContext is a
SettingsViewModel with properties: string Theme, bool NotificationsEnabled,
and int FontSize. Convert this page to use compiled bindings and tell me
how to catch binding errors at build time.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI ContentPage called SettingsPage.xaml that currently uses
plain bindings with no x:DataType. The page's BindingContext is a
SettingsViewModel with properties: string Theme, bool NotificationsEnabled,
and int FontSize. Convert this page to use compiled bindings and tell me
how to catch binding errors at build time.

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to:
1. Find their SettingsPage.xaml file
2. Convert it to use compiled bindings (x:DataType)
3. Explain how to catch binding errors at build time

Let me first find the file.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             xmlns:vm="clr-namespace:MyApp.ViewModels"
             x:Class="MyApp.Views.SettingsPage"
             x:DataType="vm:SettingsViewModel"
             Title="Settings">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="15">

        <Label Text="Theme" FontAttributes="Bold" />
        <Entry Text="{Binding Theme}" Placeholder="e.g. Light or Dark" />

        <Label Text="Notifications" FontAttributes="Bold" />
        <Switch IsToggled="{Binding NotificationsEnabled}" />

        <Label Text="Font Size" FontAttributes="Bold" />
        <Slider Minimum="8" Maximum="32"
                Value="{Binding FontSize}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding FontSize, StringFormat='Current size: {0}'}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>

</ContentPage>

    </VerticalStackLayout>

</ContentPage>

data.content:
Here's what was done and how it works:

### Key change: `x:DataType`

The single critical addition is on the `<ContentPage>` element:

```xml
xmlns:vm="clr-namespace:MyApp.ViewModels"
x:DataType="vm:SettingsViewModel"
```

This tells the XAML compiler the type of `BindingContext`, so every `{Binding ...}` on that page is **validated and compiled at build time**. A typo like `{Binding Themee}` will now produce a **build error**, not a silent runtime failure.

### Catching binding errors at build time

1. **`x:DataType` is all you need** — the MAUI XAML compiler automatically emits errors for properties that don't exist on the declared type.

2. **Add this to your `.csproj`** to ensure no XAML escapes compilation:
   ```xml
   <MauiStrictXamlCompilation>true</MauiStrictXamlCompilation>
   ```

3. **Opt out selectively** with `x:DataType="{x:Null}"` on any subtree where you genuinely need untyped/dynamic bindings.

4. **Treat warnings as errors** (optional but recommended):
   ```xml
   <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
   <!-- or just the binding-related ones: -->
   <WarningsAsErrors>XC0045;XC0022</WarningsAsErrors>
   ```

Compiled bindings are also faster at runtime since they skip reflection-based property lookup.
